last_excerpt: to put the peel back into itself the way it came off, you can only half-reconstruct the sphere, loose, never whole. --- Door hinges that stick on humid days A scrap of paper used as a bookmark, curling at one corner A clock ticking slightly out of rhythm with your pulse The slant of sunlight on a tiled floor, making the grout appear darker The way plastic bags balloon out on the sidewalk when it’s windy A light switch that clicks twice, too quick Faint sound of water rushing through pipes behind a wall The sticker residue left after peeling something off glass --- When I was younger I thought oranges were more complicated than apples because you needed patience to get to the inside. An apple is immediate, no layers to cross. The hesitation before breaking the skin. Sometimes the first taste is a little bitter from the pith.
last_concepts: the handwriting of someone you used to know
peeling an orange in one piece
